# Data Dictionary Web App - Roadmap

## Context

Build a demo-quality **Data Dictionary Web Application** that looks and feels like a polished SaaS product. The app lets businesses import dictionaries from multiple sources, define their own terms, search across all definitions, and use LLM to generate consolidated industry-aligned definitions. It will be hosted publicly so anyone with a link can use it.

The repo (`reg-scorecard`) is currently empty - just `.gitignore` and `README.md`.

---

## Recommended Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology | Why |
|-------|-----------|-----|
| **Frontend** | Next.js 14 (App Router) + TypeScript | SSR, file-based routing, deploys to Vercel in one click |
| **UI** | shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS | Professional SaaS look out of the box, dark mode, data tables |
| **Backend** | FastAPI (Python) | Matches repo's Python orientation, async, auto-generated API docs |
| **Database** | SQLite (MVP) → PostgreSQL (deploy) | Zero-config locally, single env var change to switch |
| **ORM** | SQLModel | Same models work for SQLite and PostgreSQL |
| **LLM** | Anthropic Claude API (Haiku for cost) | Excellent at synthesizing multiple definitions into one |
| **Dictionary APIs** | Free Dictionary API (no key needed) + Merriam-Webster (free tier, 1k/day) | Two complementary sources, both free |
| **PDF Processing** | PyMuPDF4LLM | Extracts PDF → Markdown in ~0.14s, feeds directly to LLM |
| **Hosting** | Vercel (frontend) + Railway (backend + DB) | Free tiers, auto-deploy from git, shareable URLs |

---

## Data Model

```
Term (id, word, normalized_word, flag_status, is_custom, category, created_at, updated_at)
  └─< Definition (id, term_id, source_id, text, part_of_speech, is_llm_generated,
                   llm_prompt_context, is_primary, created_at)
Source (id, name, type[online|user_defined|pdf_import|llm_generated], url, is_active)
  └─< Definition
ImportJob (id, source_id, status, file_name, terms_imported, errors)
LLMRefinement (id, definition_id, user_prompt, previous_text, new_text, created_at)
```

---

## Project Structure

```
backend/
  app/
    main.py, config.py, database.py
    models/    (term.py, flag.py, import_job.py)
    schemas/   (term.py, definition.py)
    routers/   (terms.py, definitions.py, sources.py, imports.py, llm.py)
    services/  (dictionary_service.py, llm_service.py, pdf_service.py, term_service.py)
    seed/      (sample_terms.json)
  tests/
  requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, Dockerfile

frontend/
  app/
    layout.tsx          (sidebar nav, header with search)
    page.tsx            (dashboard)
    terms/page.tsx      (searchable term index)
    terms/[id]/page.tsx (term detail + definitions)
    import/page.tsx     (import sources)
    search/page.tsx     (global search)
  components/
    ui/                 (shadcn/ui components)
    term-card.tsx, definition-panel.tsx, source-badge.tsx,
    flag-indicator.tsx, search-bar.tsx, import-dropzone.tsx,
    llm-refinement-dialog.tsx, sidebar-nav.tsx
  lib/
    api.ts, types.ts

docker-compose.yml
```

---

## Phased Roadmap

### Phase 1: Foundation & MVP

**Goal:** Working app with term management, one dictionary source, and a polished UI shell.

**Backend:**
- Initialize FastAPI project with SQLModel + SQLite
- Create all data models (Term, Definition, Source, ImportJob, LLMRefinement)
- CRUD endpoints for Terms (`GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/terms`, `/api/terms/{id}`)
- CRUD endpoints for Definitions (`GET/POST /api/terms/{id}/definitions`)
- Integrate Free Dictionary API: `POST /api/lookup/{word}` fetches and stores definitions
- Seed database with 20-30 common business terms (process, procedure, framework, policy, governance, compliance, risk, control, etc.)
- CORS middleware

**Frontend:**
- Initialize Next.js 14 + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui
- App shell: collapsible sidebar, header with global search, breadcrumbs
- **Dashboard page:** total terms, terms by status, recent terms, quick search
- **Terms Index page:** data t...[truncated]

from datetime import datetime, timezone

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from sqlmodel import col, func, select

from app.database import get_session
from app.models.term import Definition, FlagStatus, Source, Term
from app.schemas.term import (
    DashboardStats,
    DefinitionCreate,
    DefinitionRead,
    FlagUpdate,
    TermCreate,
    TermRead,
    TermSummary,
    TermUpdate,
)

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/terms", tags=["terms"])


@router.get("", response_model=list[TermSummary])
async def list_terms(
    q: str = Query("", description="Search query"),
    flag_status: FlagStatus | None = None,
    category: str | None = None,
    is_custom: bool | None = None,
    skip: int = 0,
    limit: int = 50,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
):
    query = select(Term)

    if q:
        query = query.where(col(Term.normalized_word).contains(q.lower()))
    if flag_status:
        query = query.where(Term.flag_status == flag_status)
    if category:
        query = query.where(Term.category == category)
    if is_custom is not None:
        query = query.where(Term.is_custom == is_custom)

    query = query.order_by(col(Term.word)).offset(skip).limit(limit)
    result = await session.execute(query)
    terms = result.scalars().all()

    summaries = []
    for term in terms:
        count_q = select(func.count()).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
        count_result = await session.execute(count_q)
        def_count = count_result.scalar() or 0
        summaries.append(
            TermSummary(
                id=term.id,
                word=term.word,
                flag_status=term.flag_status,
                is_custom=term.is_custom,
                category=term.category,
                definition_count=def_count,
                created_at=term.created_at,
                updated_at=term.updated_at,
            )
        )

    return summaries


@router.get("/stats", response_model=DashboardStats)
async def get_stats(session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)):
    total = (await session.execute(select(func.count(Term.id)))).scalar() or 0
    approved = (
        await session.execute(
            select(func.count(Term.id)).where(
                Term.flag_status == FlagStatus.approved
            )
        )
    ).scalar() or 0
    needs_review = (
        await session.execute(
            select(func.count(Term.id)).where(
                Term.flag_status == FlagStatus.needs_review
            )
        )
    ).scalar() or 0
    custom = (
        await session.execute(
            select(func.count(Term.id)).where(Term.is_custom == True)  # noqa: E712
        )
    ).scalar() or 0
    total_defs = (
        await session.execute(select(func.count(Definition.id)))
    ).scalar() or 0
    total_sources = (
        await session.execute(select(func.count(Source.id)))
    ).scalar() or 0

    recent_q = select(Term).order_by(col(Term.created_at).desc()).limit(5)
    recent_result = await session.execute(recent_q)
    recent_terms = []
    for term in recent_result.scalars().all():
        count_q = select(func.count()).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
        count_result = await session.execute(count_q)
        def_count = count_result.scalar() or 0
        recent_terms.append(
            TermSummary(
                id=term.id,
                word=term.word,
                flag_status=term.flag_status,
                is_custom=term.is_custom,
                category=term.category,
                definition_count=def_count,
                created_at=term.created_at,
                updated_at=term.updated_at,
            )
        )

    return DashboardStats(
        total_terms=total,
        approved_terms=approved,
        needs_review_terms=needs_review,
        custom_terms=custom,
        total_definitions=total_defs,
        total_sources=total_sources,
        recent_terms=recent_terms,

import asyncio

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from sqlmodel import select

from app.database import get_session
from app.models.term import Definition, Source, SourceType, Term
from app.schemas.term import DefinitionRead, LookupResult, SourceRead
from app.services.dictionary_service import DICTIONARY_SOURCES

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/lookup", tags=["lookup"])


@router.post("/{word}", response_model=LookupResult)
async def lookup_word(word: str, session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)):
    """Look up a word across all active dictionary sources and store new definitions."""
    normalized = word.lower().strip()

    # Find or create the term
    result = await session.execute(
        select(Term).where(Term.normalized_word == normalized)
    )
    term = result.scalars().first()
    if not term:
        term = Term(word=word, normalized_word=normalized)
        session.add(term)
        await session.flush()

    # Query all dictionary sources concurrently
    tasks = {}
    for source_name, lookup_fn in DICTIONARY_SOURCES.items():
        tasks[source_name] = asyncio.create_task(lookup_fn(word))

    new_count = 0
    sources_searched = []

    for source_name, task in tasks.items():
        try:
            definitions = await task
        except Exception:
            continue

        sources_searched.append(source_name)

        if not definitions:
            continue

        # Get or create the source record
        source_result = await session.execute(
            select(Source).where(Source.name == source_name)
        )
        source = source_result.scalars().first()
        if not source:
            source = Source(name=source_name, type=SourceType.online)
            session.add(source)
            await session.flush()

        # Check existing definitions from this source for this term
        existing = await session.execute(
            select(Definition).where(
                Definition.term_id == term.id,
                Definition.source_id == source.id,
            )
        )
        existing_texts = {d.text for d in existing.scalars().all()}

        for defn in definitions:
            if defn["text"] not in existing_texts:
                definition = Definition(
                    term_id=term.id,
                    source_id=source.id,
                    text=defn["text"],
                    part_of_speech=defn.get("part_of_speech"),
                    example=defn.get("example"),
                )
                session.add(definition)
                new_count += 1

    await session.commit()

    # Return all definitions for this term
    defs_result = await session.execute(
        select(Definition).where(Definition.term_id == term.id)
    )
    all_defs = defs_result.scalars().all()
    def_reads = []
    for d in all_defs:
        source = None
        if d.source_id:
            src_result = await session.execute(
                select(Source).where(Source.id == d.source_id)
            )
            source = src_result.scalars().first()
        def_reads.append(
            DefinitionRead(
                id=d.id,
                term_id=d.term_id,
                source_id=d.source_id,
                text=d.text,
                part_of_speech=d.part_of_speech,
                example=d.example,
                is_llm_generated=d.is_llm_generated,
                is_primary=d.is_primary,
                source=SourceRead.model_validate(source) if source else None,
                created_at=d.created_at,
            )
        )

    return LookupResult(
        word=word,
        definitions=def_reads,
        sources_searched=sources_searched,
        new_definitions_added=new_count,
    )

import json
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from pathlib import Path

from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from sqlmodel import select

from app.config import settings
from app.database import async_session, engine, init_db
from app.models.term import Definition, Source, SourceType, Term
from app.routers import lookup, sources, terms


async def seed_database():
    """Seed the database with sample terms if empty."""
    async with async_session() as session:
        result = await session.execute(select(Term))
        if result.scalars().first() is not None:
            return

        # Create the "User Defined" source
        user_source = Source(name="User Defined", type=SourceType.user_defined)
        session.add(user_source)

        # Create the dictionary sources
        free_dict_source = Source(
            name="Free Dictionary",
            type=SourceType.online,
            url="https://dictionaryapi.dev/",
        )
        session.add(free_dict_source)

        mw_source = Source(
            name="Merriam-Webster",
            type=SourceType.online,
            url="https://dictionaryapi.com/",
        )
        session.add(mw_source)

        await session.flush()

        seed_file = Path(__file__).parent / "seed" / "sample_terms.json"
        with open(seed_file) as f:
            sample_terms = json.load(f)

        for item in sample_terms:
            term = Term(
                word=item["word"],
                normalized_word=item["word"].lower().strip(),
                is_custom=True,
                category=item.get("category"),
                flag_status="needs_review",
            )
            session.add(term)
            await session.flush()

            definition = Definition(
                term_id=term.id,
                source_id=user_source.id,
                text=item["definition"],
                is_primary=True,
            )
            session.add(definition)

        await session.commit()
        print(f"Seeded {len(sample_terms)} terms")


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    # Create data directory
    Path("data").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    await init_db()
    await seed_database()
    yield
    await engine.dispose()


app = FastAPI(
    title="Data Dictionary API",
    description="A comprehensive data dictionary management tool",
    version="0.1.0",
    lifespan=lifespan,
)

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=settings.allowed_origins.split(","),
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

app.include_router(terms.router)
app.include_router(lookup.router)
app.include_router(sources.router)


@app.get("/api/health")
async def health():
    return {"status": "ok", "version": "0.1.0"}

file: /home/user/reg-scorecard/README.md

# DataDict - Data Dictionary Web App

A comprehensive data dictionary management tool that aggregates definitions from multiple sources, supports business-specific terminology, and provides AI-powered definition synthesis.

## Features

- **Multi-source dictionary lookup** - Search terms across Free Dictionary API, Merriam-Webster, and more
- **Business data dictionary** - Define and manage custom business terms with categories and flags
- **Searchable term index** - Browse, filter, and search all terms with status tracking
- **Definition aggregation** - View all definitions for a term grouped by source
- **Flagging system** - Flag terms as approved, needs review, disputed, or archived
- **Dark mode** - Full dark/light theme support
- **Responsive design** - Works on desktop, tablet, and mobile

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Frontend | Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4 |
| Backend | FastAPI, Python 3.12, SQLModel, SQLite |
| State | TanStack React Query |
| UI | Custom components (shadcn/ui-inspired) |

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

- Python 3.11+
- Node.js 18+
- npm

### Backend

```bash
cd backend
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
uvicorn app.main:app --reload
```

The API will be available at http://localhost:8000. Visit http://localhost:8000/docs for the interactive API docs.

### Frontend

```bash
cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev
```

The app will be available at http://localhost:3000.

### Docker Compose

```bash
docker-compose up
```

Both services will start - frontend at http://localhost:3000, backend at http://localhost:8000.

## Project Structure

```
backend/
  app/
    main.py              # FastAPI application entry point
    config.py            # Environment configuration
    database.py          # Database engine and session
    models/term.py       # SQLModel data models
    schemas/term.py      # Pydantic API schemas
    routers/
      terms.py           # Term CRUD + flagging endpoints
      lookup.py          # External dictionary lookup
      sources.py         # Dictionary source management
    services/
      dictionary_service.py  # External API integrations
    seed/
      sample_terms.json  # Initial business terms
  requirements.txt
  Dockerfile

frontend/
  app/
    layout.tsx           # Root layout with sidebar
    page.tsx             # Dashboard
    terms/page.tsx       # Terms index
    terms/[id]/page.tsx  # Term detail
    search/page.tsx      # Global search + lookup
  components/
    ui/                  # Reusable UI components
    sidebar-nav.tsx      # Navigation sidebar
    add-term-dialog.tsx  # New term form
    flag-badge.tsx       # Status badges
    source-badge.tsx     # Source type badges
  lib/
    api.ts               # API client functions
    types.ts             # TypeScript type definitions
```

## Roadmap

See [ROADMAP.md](./ROADMAP.md) for the full phased development roadmap.

Everything is committed and pushed. Here's a summary of what was built:

---

## What's Done

### Phase 1 MVP - Built & Pushed

**Backend** (FastAPI + Python):
- **5 data models**: Term, Definition, Source, ImportJob, LLMRefinement
- **Full REST API**: CRUD for terms, definitions, flagging, dashboard stats
- **Dictionary lookup**: Free Dictionary API integrated (no key needed), Merriam-Webster ready (needs key)
- **25 seeded business terms**: governance, risk, compliance, process, framework, etc.
- **Auto-generated API docs** at `/docs`

**Frontend** (Next.js 15 + React 19 + Tailwind v4):
- **Dashboard**: stat cards (total terms, approved, needs review, custom, definitions, sources) + recent terms list
- **Terms Index**: searchable data table with status/category filtering, "Add Term" dialog
- **Term Detail**: definitions grouped by source with color-coded badges, "Lookup" button to fetch external definitions, flag status dropdown
- **Search**: global search with live matching + external dictionary lookup
- **Polish**: collapsible sidebar, dark/light mode, skeleton loading states, responsive layout, custom shadcn-inspired UI components

**Infrastructure**: Docker Compose, Dockerfiles, comprehensive README

### Roadmap (in `ROADMAP.md`)

| Phase | Focus | Key Features |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| **1** (done) | Foundation & MVP | Term CRUD, dictionary lookup, polished UI |
| **2** | LLM Integration | AI-generated consolidated definitions, refinement workflow (open-source LLM) |
| **3** | Import & Advanced Search | CSV/JSON import, full-text search, export, bulk actions |
| **4** | PDF Import & Demo Polish | PDF extraction, glossary detection, onboarding tour, demo mode |


# Data Dictionary - Move & Run Plan

## Context

The data dictionary app we built earlier lived on branch `claude/data-dictionary-roadmap-XQhD8` in the `gavintreseder/reg-scorecard` repo. The branch has since been reset to the reg-scorecard project code — so **the data dictionary code no longer exists in this repo** (neither in working tree nor git history here).

The user wants:
1. To "move" the data dictionary out of the wrong repo
2. A place to **run it and see it working** — a shareable URL they can open in a browser

Key constraint: Claude Code web runs in a container with no port forwarding to the user's browser. `localhost` links don't work. We need either a remote deploy or a service with built-in public URLs.

---

## Current State

| Item | Status |
|------|--------|
| Data dictionary source code in repo | ❌ Gone (branch reset) |
| Committed in git history here | ❌ No |
| Can create new GitHub repos from Claude Code | ❌ Git proxy only authorizes `reg-scorecard` |
| Can push to `gavintreseder/reg-scorecard` branch | ✅ Yes |
| Has working scaffold design (known good) | ✅ Yes - we built it before |

---

## Hosting Options Comparison

| Platform | How you'd view it | Setup complexity | Free tier | Best for |
|----------|------------------|------------------|-----------|----------|
| **GitHub Codespaces** | Browser-based VS Code with auto port-forwarding (click "Open in Browser" on port 3000) | Lowest — push code, open codespace | 60 hrs/mo free | **Fastest to see working** |
| **Gitpod** | Same as Codespaces — port-forwarded preview in browser | Low — needs `.gitpod.yml` | 50 hrs/mo free | Same as above |
| **Vercel + Railway** | Public URL like `https://your-app.vercel.app` | Medium — split frontend/backend | Both have free tiers | Production-like demo |
| **Railway (single service)** | Public URL like `https://app.up.railway.app` | Medium — needs single-container setup | $5/mo after free trial | Simplest prod URL |
| **Render** | Public URL | Medium — similar to Railway | Free tier (cold starts) | Alt to Railway |
| **Replit** | Public URL, browser IDE | Lowest — import from GitHub URL | Free (sleeps when idle) | Quick shareable demo |
| **Local on your machine** | `localhost:3000` on your own PC | Lowest — just clone & run | N/A | No sharing, full control |

---

## Recommended Path

Given "I want to see it running" is the priority, and you don't have a new repo yet, I recommend a **two-stage approach**:

### Stage 1: Pick a home for the code

Since I can't create a new GitHub repo from here, pick **one** of:

**A.** You create `gavintreseder/data-dictionary` on github.com (30 seconds in browser), grant Claude Code access in a new session. I rebuild the app there cleanly.

**B.** Keep it here on a clearly-named branch (e.g., `claude/data-dictionary-app-XQhD8`) on the reg-scorecard repo, ignoring that the repo name doesn't match. Easiest to proceed right now.

**C.** I produce a tarball of the code in this session. You download it, create the repo yourself, push it, deploy where you like.

### Stage 2: Pick how you want to view it

**For fastest "see it working":** GitHub Codespaces on whatever repo you pick.
- Push the code
- Open the repo on github.com
- Click "Code" → "Codespaces" → "Create codespace"
- VS Code opens in your browser
- `npm run dev` (frontend) + `uvicorn app.main:app` (backend) in two terminals
- VS Code auto-prompts you with a "Open in Browser" link when port 3000 opens
- **Public URL gets generated automatically by Codespaces**

**For shareable permanent URL:** Vercel + Railway deploy.
- Frontend (Next.js) → Vercel (free, 1-click from GitHub)
- Backend (FastAPI) → Railway (free tier, 1-click from GitHub)
- DB → Railway's built-in Postgres

**For one-click demo:** Replit import.
- Copy repo URL into Replit "Import from GitHub"
- It runs automatically and gives you a `.repl.co` URL

---

Three fixes: background the DB seed, drop railway.toml's startCommand (so the Dockerfile's `sh -c` shell handles ${PORT}), bump healthcheck timeout 30s → 120s.

blocks.summary: main.py: replaced sync seed with asyncio.create_task(_background_seed()) so /api/health is reachable in ~1s
railway.toml: removed startCommand; relies on Dockerfile CMD with sh -c to expand ${PORT}
Pushed to feature branch; fast-forwarded main; pushed main

messages.summary: That worked — but the regex isn't finding terms well in PDFs. Would an LLM RAG approach work? Can you use Hugging Face?
Clarified it's not RAG, just LLM-based extraction. Added an LLM extractor (chunked + JSON-mode) with regex fallback, documented free HF token setup.

blocks.text: Added `extract_terms()` in llm_service.py that chunks text into ~6k-char windows with 400-char overlap, prompts the LLM for STRICT JSON with a tight system prompt, and tolerates code fences / preamble / trailing commas in the response. PDF extractor now runs LLM first and merges in regex hits to cover anything LLM drops. When no LLM is configured the regex pass remains. Preview API returns an `extractor` label so the UI can show which path ran (`llm`, `llm+regex`, `regex`).

blocks.summary: pdf_extractor.py: extract_from_pdf_bytes_async() runs LLM + regex and merges
import_export router: switched to async path, surfaces `extractor` label
Schema: PDFExtraction.extractor field; frontend types + UI to show it
Unit-tested chunker + JSON parser on tricky cases
README: documented HF token setup at huggingface.co/settings/tokens

messages.summary: It had llm=true but still dropped back to regex.
Root cause: HF deprecated the old api-inference endpoint for instruct models in 2025. Added Groq backend, rewrote HF to use router.huggingface.co chat completions, surfaced LLM errors in the UI.

blocks.text: Rewrote the LLM service around a single OpenAI-compatible helper. Three backends, preference order Groq → Ollama → HF. `_invoke()` records every failure reason. The PDF preview API surfaces the first 3 errors so the UI can show 'LLM extraction failed — fell back to regex. Cause: <exact HTTP error>'. Added Groq config (GROQ_API_KEY, GROQ_MODEL) and HF_PROVIDER. README documents all three with Groq as the recommended path.

blocks.summary: config.py: added GROQ_API_KEY/GROQ_MODEL/HF_PROVIDER; llm_enabled() includes Groq
llm_service.py rewrite: _call_openai_compat helper; Groq + HF + Ollama backends through one path
extract_terms returns errors; PDFExtraction.llm_errors surfaces them
Frontend: yellow banner in PDF preview showing the failure reason
/api/system shows groq/ollama/huggingface flags + active model label

messages.summary: How do I add Groq with a fallback to other LLM?
Already built — fallback chain is automatic per-call. Just set GROQ_API_KEY and HF_API_TOKEN; the code tries Groq first, falls through on failure.
Got a Groq token error.

Identified it as the daily token budget. Reduced per-PDF token usage ~75% (chunks 6k→10k chars, max_tokens 1400→800), added 429-aware blacklist so Groq drops out for the rest of the batch instead of being retried per chunk.

blocks.summary: _invoke accepts skip:set; appends to skip on 429/'rate'/'quota'/'token' errors
extract_terms threads skip across chunks; bails if every backend skipped
README: 'Surviving Groq's free tier rate limits' section
